focus: Verify implementation-summary/resource-map claims against disk state, runtime command mirrors, and advisor rename references

findingDetails.title: Resource map marks nonexistent runtime command mirrors as OK
findingDetails.evidence: resource-map.md claims Missing on disk: 0 and marks Codex command files plus Gemini YAML command assets as OK, while disk inventory shows .codex/commands/deep is absent and .gemini/commands/deep contains only README.txt and improve-agent.toml; implementation-summary.md lines 87-88 already document those shapes correctly.
findingDetails.recommendation: Revise resource-map runtime command mirror rows and summary so Gemini YAML assets and Codex command files are N/A/absent rather than OK.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Direct disk inventory and resource-map rows show the mismatch is confined to runtime command mirror evidence rows for Gemini assets and Codex commands.

noveltyJustification: First correctness iteration found one new P1 evidence-matrix mismatch; no prior iteration findings existed.

traceabilityChecks.notes: Checked implementation-summary/resource-map runtime mirror claims against disk and sampled advisor source rename references.

edgeCases: Resource map appears partly authored as a planning inventory; classified active because packet treats it as first-class release evidence.
ruledOut: Absent .codex/commands/deep is not a runtime implementation failure because implementation-summary documents it as N/A.
Absent Gemini YAML assets are not a runtime implementation failure because implementation-summary documents no YAML assets in that dir.
